whingeing.definition: Making continuous, annoying crying or complaining sounds (British).
whingeing.example: The child's whingeing was hard for the parents to handle.

whining.definition: Complaining in a high, annoying voice.
whining.example: Whining is one behaviour that really bothers parents.

clinging.definition: Holding on tightly and not wanting to let go or be apart.
clinging.example: His clinging behaviour worried his mother.

plaintive.definition: Sounding sad and as if asking for sympathy.
plaintive.example: The child made a plaintive sound.

grizzling.definition: Crying softly and continuously in an unhappy way.
grizzling.example: The baby was grizzling all morning.

offspring.example: Parents can't always understand why their offspring are sad.

heroic.definition: Very brave or requiring great effort.
heroic.example: Despite heroic efforts to cheer him up, he was still sad.

distract.definition: To take someone's attention away from something.
distract.example: She tried to distract the crying child with a toy.

irritation.example: The irritation you feel may come from your own past.

infant.example: She remembered what it felt like when she was an infant.

defenceless.example: As an infant, you felt sad and defenceless.

annoyance.definition: The feeling of being slightly angry or bothered.
annoyance.example: Your annoyance may come from old feelings.

fragile.example: Old feelings of fragile vulnerability can resurface.

vulnerability.definition: The state of being easily hurt emotionally or physically.
vulnerability.example: Parents may not want to feel their own vulnerability.

criticism.definition: Words or actions that show you think something is wrong or bad.
criticism.example: You may feel your child's crying is a criticism of your parenting.

impotence.definition: The feeling of being unable to do anything about a situation.
impotence.example: The whining reminded her of her impotence in that moment.

loneliness.definition: The sad feeling of being alone.
loneliness.example: What the child felt was just sadness or loneliness.

corporation.example: She works as a senior manager in a large corporation.

restaurateur.definition: A person who owns and runs a restaurant.
restaurateur.example: Steve is a chef and restaurateur.

lively.example: They are a lively family with many activities.

socializing.definition: Spending time with other people in a friendly way.

atmosphere.definition: The feeling or mood of a place.
atmosphere.example: The atmosphere at home was cheerful.

demanding.definition: Needing a lot of time, effort, or attention.

childminder.definition: A person paid to look after children in their home.
childminder.example: Juanita works as their childminder during the week.

clingy.definition: Always wanting to be close to someone and not wanting to be apart.
clingy.example: Felix is really clingy and needs extra attention.

insecure.example: She wondered why Felix seemed so insecure.

bond.definition: A strong feeling of connection between people.
bond.example: She wondered if she didn't bond properly with him as a baby.

stuck.definition: Unable to move forward or change.
stuck.example: Something was stuck in their relationship.

nightmare.definition: A scary or very bad dream.
nightmare.example: Felix had a terrible nightmare about being alone.

confident.definition: Sure that something is true or that you can do something.
confident.example: She was confident he had never been left alone.

devastated.example: She was devastated that he remembered that incident.

incident.definition: Something that happens, especially something unusual or bad.
incident.example: The incident in the car stayed with Felix for years.

severe.example: He had a severe infection that put him in the hospital.

septic.example: The septic throat required hospital treatment.

hospitalized.definition: Put into a hospital for medical treatment.
hospitalized.example: He had to be hospitalized for a week.

antibiotics.example: The antibiotics didn't work at first.

coma.definition: A deep state of being unconscious, often from illness or injury.
coma.example: He was put in a coma to help him recover.

resonated.definition: Had a strong meaning or emotional effect on someone.

repressed.definition: Pushed painful feelings deep down so you do not think about them.
repressed.example: She had repressed her fear about Felix's illness.

insensitive.definition: Not noticing or caring about other people's feelings.
insensitive.example: Repressing your feelings can make you insensitive to others.

consume.definition: To completely take over or use up.
consume.example: She feared the feelings would consume her.

irony.definition: A situation that is strange because it is the opposite of what you expect.
irony.example: The irony was that she was now the one checking on Felix.

drowned.definition: Died from being unable to breathe under water.
drowned.example: In the dream, Felix drowned in the sea.

clinginess.definition: The behaviour of always wanting to be close to someone.
clinginess.example: There are many reasons for clinginess in children.

sensitive.definition: Able to notice and care about feelings easily.
sensitive.example: Not being sensitive to feelings makes relationships difficult.

trigger.definition: Something that causes a feeling or reaction to start.
trigger.example: Understanding your triggers helps you respond better.

capacity.definition: The ability to do or experience something.
capacity.example: It reduces our children's capacity for happiness.

acknowledging.definition: Accepting and recognizing something as real and important.
acknowledging.example: Acknowledging feelings is important for mental health.

validating.definition: Showing that someone's feelings are real and understandable.
validating.example: Validating feelings helps build deeper connections.

mental health.definition: The condition of your mind and emotions.
mental health.example: Good mental health comes from having your feelings accepted.

ghosts.definition: Spirits of dead people that some believe can appear.
ghosts.example: Children's fears of ghosts in the wardrobe make sense.

monsters.example: Fear of monsters under the bed is normal for children.

overwhelming.definition: So strong that it is hard to deal with.
overwhelming.example: Overwhelming frustration needs to be understood.

context.definition: The situation or background that helps explain something.
context.example: All feelings make sense once we find their context.

obvious.definition: Easy to see or understand; clear.

tolerate.definition: To accept something difficult without complaining.
tolerate.example: Once you understand the feeling, you can tolerate the behaviour.

collaborate.example: Collaborate with your child to find solutions.
